(mock brain - limited) I can do exact trouble-code lookups, oil capacity, and wiring-diagram sources offline right now. For full top-5 diagnostics and step-by-step repair help, add an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to .env to switch on my full brain.

P0300: Random/Multiple Cylinder Misfire Detected  [system: Ignition/Fuel | severity: high]
  Common causes:
   - Worn spark plugs across the board (most common, especially overdue maintenance)
   - Vacuum leak causing lean misfire across cylinders
   - Low fuel pressure (weak pump, restricted filter)
   - Failing ignition coil(s) — modern COP setups can have multiple weak coils
   - Bad fuel (water, contamination)
   - Carbon-fouled valves on direct-injection engines
   - EGR valve stuck open
  Diagnostic steps:
   1. Pull all plugs, inspect for wear, fouling, or fuel contamination
   2. Check fuel pressure — both static and under load
   3. Smoke test intake for vacuum leaks
   4. Scan misfire counts per cylinder to look for clustering
   5. Inspect EGR valve operation if equipped
  Ask the customer/tech:
   - Misfire counts spread evenly or clustered? Even spread points to fuel/vacuum/EGR. Clustering points to one bank or one coil pack.
   - Recent fuel fill-up? Bad fuel is more common than people think.
   - Last plug change interval — has it ever been done?
  Shop notes: Direct-injection engines (GDI/TDI): valve carbon buildup causes P0300 plus rough cold start. Walnut blasting is the fix. Common on VW/Audi 2.0T, BMW N54/N55, Hyundai Theta II GDI.

lug torque for a ram promaster

## RAM ProMaster — 2014–on (Fiat Ducato platform)
*Also sold as Fiat Ducato / Peugeot Boxer / Citroën Jumper*
- Lug nut torque — **145 ft-lb** per owner's manual [1-SRC]. Many techs run 100–120 by preference; spec is 145. Use pilot/guide pins to hang the wheel.
- Front caliper adapter/bracket bolt — **~125 ft-lb** (2015 1500 3.6) [1-SRC, CONFIRM by year]
- Caliper guide-pin bolts — **[CONFIRM]** not pinned; verify in service info
- *Expand: tighttorque.com has a fuller ProMaster table to confirm against*

## FORD F-150 — 2015–2020 (aluminum body) — Brakes & wheels
*Source: Ford owner's manual figure + multiple F-150 references. [CORROB] on lug; [1-SRC] on bracket*
- Lug nut torque — **150 ft-lb** (M14×1.5 studs); Ford specifies re-torque after ~100 miles
  - Older F-150s with **12 mm studs — 100 ft-lb**. Confirm which stud you have.
- Front caliper bracket bolts — **~184 ft-lb** (high; often one-time-use — replace) [CONFIRM exact by sub-model]
- Caliper guide/slide bolts — **~27 ft-lb**
- Front hub mounting bolts — **129 ft-lb**
- Outer tie rod nut — **85 ft-lb**; inner tie rod — **70 ft-lb**

P0741: Torque Converter Clutch Performance/Stuck Off
  Common causes:
   - Faulty TCC solenoid
   - Valve body
   - Low fluid

P0496: EVAP System Flow During Non-Purge Condition (GM-specific common)  [system: EVAP | severity: low]
  Common causes:
   - Stuck-open EVAP purge valve
   - Damaged purge valve solenoid
   - Vacuum leak in EVAP system between purge valve and intake
  Diagnostic steps:
   1. Test purge valve operation with scanner
   2. Inspect purge valve and lines
   3. Replace purge valve — usually the answer on GM
  Ask the customer/tech:
   - Stall after fill-up or at idle?
   - GM vehicle? (P0496 is heavily GM-flavored.)
  Shop notes: On GM trucks and crossovers (Silverado, Equinox, Traverse, etc.) P0496 = bad purge solenoid 90% of the time. Cheap part, easy replacement, customer happy.

P0300: Random/Multiple Cylinder Misfire Detected  [system: Ignition/Fuel | severity: high]
  Common causes:
   - Worn spark plugs across the board (most common, especially overdue maintenance)
   - Vacuum leak causing lean misfire across cylinders
   - Low fuel pressure (weak pump, restricted filter)
   - Failing ignition coil(s) — modern COP setups can have multiple weak coils
   - Bad fuel (water, contamination)
   - Carbon-fouled valves on direct-injection engines
   - EGR valve stuck open
  Diagnostic steps:
   1. Pull all plugs, inspect for wear, fouling, or fuel contamination
   2. Check fuel pressure — both static and under load
   3. Smoke test intake for vacuum leaks
   4. Scan misfire counts per cylinder to look for clustering
   5. Inspect EGR valve operation if equipped
  Ask the customer/tech:
   - Misfire counts spread evenly or clustered? Even spread points to fuel/vacuum/EGR. Clustering points to one bank or one coil pack.
   - Recent fuel fill-up? Bad fuel is more common than people think.
   - Last plug change interval — has it ever been done?
  Shop notes: Direct-injection engines (GDI/TDI): valve carbon buildup causes P0300 plus rough cold start. Walnut blasting is the fix. Common on VW/Audi 2.0T, BMW N54/N55, Hyundai Theta II GDI.
